mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing a negative drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as FEDFUNDS remains above its trailing 12-month average.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during high-rate periods are not significantly more negative than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points from its 6-month trailing low (Sahm-rule style trigger), it signals an inflection into labor market deterioration that historically precedes equity drawdowns over the subsequent quarter, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above its trailing 6-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE currently at 3.5, near cycle lows
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 across a 3-year rolling walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects synchronized consumer and construction demand strength that jointly forecasts continued economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; neither series alone captures this joint-strength signal because retail spending and housing investment respond to different consumer decision horizons.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST 3-month growth rates positive simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to outperform single-series RSAFS-only signal by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.05) over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, sustained industrial production (INDPRO) growth signals resilient real-economy supply capacity that supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon, but this relationship is regime-dependent and weakens or inverts once the Fed begins easing.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO-return relationship fails to hold (Rank IC < 0.03) specifically during tightening-cycle subsamples, or the sign flips to negative in more than one of three rolling tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.0%, entering a restrictive policy regime, short-horizon equity volatility and downside risk increase due to tightening credit conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days immediately following the crossing.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following the 5.0% crossing are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or the effect disappears (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of decelerating nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) alongside a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly signals a genuine labor market turning point rather than noisy monthly volatility, and this joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days more reliably than either series individually.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth turns negative while UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes do not produce significantly more negative 20-day returns than the UNRATE-only threshold signal (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery regimes, above-trend real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals renewed aggregate demand momentum that supports forward earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon; this effect is muted or absent during steady-state mid-cycle regimes because GDP surprises carry less incremental information when growth is already stable.
failure_condition: GDPC1-return Rank IC is not meaningfully higher during identified post-shock recovery windows compared to mid-cycle windows (difference < 0.02), invalidating the regime-conditional claim.

mechanism_narrative: A steady month-over-month increase in the CPI index reflects persistent inflationary pressure that compresses real returns and raises rate-hike expectations, producing a negative drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as an always-on background effect.
failure_condition: Rank IC between CPIAUCSL month-over-month change and forward 5-day SPX returns stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, indicating no reliable short-horizon predictive relationship.
